verifier_values.criteria: States that the provided termination notice was not valid under the ENA
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: While the City is authorized to exercise the right to terminate the ENA if it ”reasonably determines that a successful consummation of the negotiations is not likely”.  It can exercise this right by giving 20 days written notice citing this reason.  Here, the notice was sent by e-mail, which is not one of the authorized means of sending legal notices under the agreement.  Therefore, the notice was not valid.

artifacts_to_reference.name: Deliverable Drafts/LOI/FINAL Exclusive Negotiating Agreement San D and Liv Dev Co.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that City is not entitled to keep entire $50,000 deposit at issue
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Under Section 3.1, the City is entitled to retain "Negotiation Expenses", consisting of public outreach, negotiating and preparing the agreement, reviewing documents relating to the project, and negotiating/drafting the Development Agreement.  Several of the claimed expenses are outside of the scope of Negotiating Expenses, including Holiday Decorations Near Subject Property ($2,500), Lost Opportunity Costs ($6,600), and Interest Paid on Property Loans ($3,400).  Therefore, once a properly issued notice is issued, the properly claimed Negotiation Expenses should be no greater than $37,500.

artifacts_to_reference.name: Deliverable Drafts/LOI/FINAL Exclusive Negotiating Agreement San D and Liv Dev Co.pdf
